steps.title: Set up a substitution to apply the functional equation
steps.content: We're given $f(4x) = x - 7$ and need to find $f(12)$. Since the left side of the equation has input $4x$, we need to find the value of $x$ that makes $4x = 12$ so we can evaluate the function at that point. Solving $4x = 12$ gives $x = 3$.

steps.title: Substitute and evaluate
steps.content: Substituting $x = 3$ into the functional equation $f(4x) = x - 7$: $$f(12) = f(4 \cdot 3) = 3 - 7 = -4$$ That matches Choice B — $-4$.
